prompt: High-fidelity portrait mobile app UI mockup for WakeSaga onboarding setup. Direction: Group Chat Pilot. A Gen Z anime social setup screen that feels like a private morning group chat, not a productivity dashboard. The screen asks one punchy onboarding question: "What steals your morning?" Show friendly chat bubbles from anime-inspired friend avatars reacting to the user's answer, a manga sunrise sticker, a small "Episode 001" badge, and 4 large tappable poll choices. Visual language: clean white canvas, candy coral, electric lime, sky blue, ink-black linework, sticker shadows, playful but polished. The CTA says "Keep going". Make it feel viral and screenshot-worthy while preserving WakeSaga's anime training-arc identity.
style: modern mobile UI mockup, editorial app-store-quality, Gen Z social app energy plus clean manga/anime accents
composition: single iPhone-sized portrait screen, no device frame, generous touch targets, big readable UI, main question high on the screen, chat/poll stack below, bottom CTA
lighting: bright morning energy, crisp UI lighting
palette: warm white, candy coral, electric lime, sky blue, lemon yellow, ink black
text: WakeSaga / Episode 001 / What steals your morning? / snooze spiral / phone scroll / chaos brain / late start / Keep going
constraints: Use short legible English UI text only. Preserve anime identity through original manga-style stickers, episode label, expressive avatars, and training-arc language. Do not copy Trip BFF branding, logos, typography, or proprietary screens.
negative: no dark RPG HUD, no cyberpunk, no dashboard, no terminal panels, no fake Japanese text, no copyrighted anime characters, no tiny paragraphs, no generic SaaS gradients, no device frame, no watermark

prompt: High-fidelity portrait mobile app UI mockup for WakeSaga onboarding setup. Direction: BFF Quiz Cards. A viral Gen Z onboarding quiz screen with bold stacked cards and expressive anime sticker energy. The screen asks: "Be honest. After your alarm, you usually..." Show 4 oversized answer cards with short casual labels, each with tiny emoji-like manga reaction stickers and color tabs. Include a playful progress strip, "WakeSaga" at top, a small "training arc setup" chip, and a bottom "Next" CTA. The design should feel social, shareable, and friendly, but still clearly about building a morning anime arc rather than planning a trip.
style: polished social quiz UI, bold card hierarchy, Gen Z stickerbook aesthetic, original anime/manga identity
composition: single iPhone-sized portrait screen, no device frame, large center question, stacked asymmetrical answer cards, sticky bottom CTA, all text readable
lighting: clean bright daylight with soft UI depth
palette: butter yellow, hot coral, mint, periwinkle, off-white, black ink
text: WakeSaga / training arc setup / Be honest. After your alarm, you usually... / snooze / scroll / rush / lock in / Next
constraints: Keep the app identity anime-motivational, not travel or friendship planning. Use original character/sticker accents only. Text must be minimal and legible.
negative: no Trip BFF logo or copied layouts, no dark fantasy panels, no cluttered HUD, no fake Japanese text, no copyrighted anime IP, no long body copy, no low-contrast text, no device frame, no watermark

prompt: High-fidelity portrait mobile app UI mockup for WakeSaga onboarding setup. Direction: Morning Persona Builder. A Gen Z anime profile-builder screen where the user creates their morning persona. Header says "Pick your wake persona". Show a large editable anime-style avatar medallion, a name/tag field reading "main character energy", chips for "study arc", "gym arc", "comeback arc", "deep work", and a section called "Your morning rival" with three playful selectable cards: "Snooze", "Doomscroll", "Chaos". Add compact friend-reaction bubbles and a bottom CTA "Build my arc". It should feel like creating a social profile for your morning training arc.
style: premium social profile setup UI, playful Gen Z creator-tool energy, original anime character design accents
composition: single iPhone-sized portrait screen, no device frame, hero avatar near top, chips and rival cards below, bottom CTA, strong hierarchy and readable text
lighting: fresh morning light, clean UI highlights
palette: cream white, vivid turquoise, cherry red, soft lavender, sunflower yellow, black line art
text: WakeSaga / Pick your wake persona / main character energy / study arc / gym arc / comeback arc / deep work / Your morning rival / Snooze / Doomscroll / Chaos / Build my arc
constraints: Make the setup feel personal and viral, but still practical for onboarding. Keep anime identity original and tasteful. No copied app brand elements.
negative: no generic SaaS profile screen, no dark RPG inventory UI, no cyberpunk, no fake Japanese text, no copyrighted anime, no tiny labels, no device frame, no watermark

prompt: High-fidelity portrait mobile app UI mockup for WakeSaga onboarding setup. Direction: Wake Receipt Setup. A Gen Z shareable setup screen that previews the user's future "Wake Receipt" as they answer onboarding questions. Header says "Tomorrow needs an opening scene". Show a central receipt/story card with bold fields: "wake time", "first enemy", "first win", "arc mood". Around it, use anime sunrise stickers, reaction stamps, colored tape, and small social proof bubbles. Include setup controls under the receipt and a bottom CTA "Set the scene". It should feel like something users would screenshot or share, while still being an alarm onboarding flow.
style: shareable social card UI, Gen Z receipt aesthetic, polished anime morning arc branding
composition: single iPhone-sized portrait screen, no device frame, central receipt card, light setup controls underneath, bottom CTA, large legible text
lighting: bright optimistic morning, soft paper texture without clutter
palette: warm white paper, bubblegum pink, cobalt blue, lime green, golden sunrise, black ink
text: WakeSaga / Tomorrow needs an opening scene / Wake Receipt / wake time / first enemy / first win / arc mood / Set the scene
constraints: Create an original WakeSaga design language. The share card should be legible and app-like, not a poster. Keep copy short.
negative: no fake receipts with random numbers, no travel app look, no dark RPG HUD, no terminal/dashboard UI, no fake Japanese text, no copyrighted anime IP, no tiny paragraphs, no device frame, no watermark
